title: Graph Algorithms: MCP Tools & Relation Weights
impact: Expose graph algorithms via MCP tools; Enable weighted relationships

tasks.description: Add Relation Weight and Metadata - Extend Relation interface with optional weight (number) and metadata (Record<string, unknown>) fields for weighted graph algorithms
acceptanceCriteria: SQLite schema updated with weight column

tasks.description: Add find_shortest_path Tool - Create MCP tool definition and handler that calls GraphTraversal.shortestPath(), accept from, to, relationTypes, and maxDepth parameters
testCategories: Returns path with entity names and relations
Returns message when no path exists
acceptanceCriteria: Returns path as entity name array and relation descriptions
Returns 'No path found' message when appropriate

tasks.description: Add get_connected_components Tool - Create MCP tool that returns component analysis, accept optional minSize filter
testCategories: Response includes entity names and counts
acceptanceCriteria: Returns component details (size, entity names, relation count)

tasks.description: Add get_centrality Tool - Create MCP tool supporting degree, betweenness, and pagerank algorithms, accept algorithm and limit parameters
acceptanceCriteria: Parameters: algorithm (enum: degree, betweenness, pagerank), limit (optional)
Returns topEntities array with entity names and scores

tasks.description: Add find_all_paths Tool - Create MCP tool to find all paths between entities with maxLength limit
acceptanceCriteria: Returns paths array with entity names and lengths
Limited to 100 paths to prevent explosion

tasks.description: Create MCP Tool Integration Tests - Test all new graph algorithm tools through MCP interface

successCriteria: 4 new MCP tools for graph algorithms
Relation weight and metadata support added
notes: This sprint completes the Graph Algorithms phase by exposing the algorithms via MCP tools. Users can now ask questions like 'how is Alice connected to the Marketing project?' or 'which entities are most central to my knowledge graph?'. The relation weight support enables future weighted shortest path algorithms.
